case: E1 resources/subscribe + polling-fallback resources/updated; subscribe advertised only when a change source is confirmed

result.instructions: Call oracle_capabilities first to discover tools, the current/max operating level, and connection status. Reads are frictionless; writes/DDL require a gated escalation.

serverInfo.description: Governed, least-privilege Oracle Database MCP server with a fail-closed SQL guard and PL/SQL intelligence.
serverInfo.name: oraclemcp
serverInfo.title: Oracle MCP server

tools.description: Compact discovery entry point: tools, operating level + gates, connection/standby status, feature tiers, version. Pass detail_level=full for the complete pre-0.9.1 descriptor report.

detail_level.description: compact (default) omits tool summaries and schemas; full returns the complete descriptor report.

tools.name: oracle_capabilities
tools.title: Oracle Capabilities

tools.description: List configured connection profiles without exposing connect strings, usernames, or credential references.
tools.name: oracle_list_profiles
tools.title: Oracle List Profiles

tools.description: Describe the active profile and Oracle connection. When live connection metadata is unavailable, returns connected=false with a structured connection_error and next_actions.
tools.name: oracle_connection_info
tools.title: Oracle Connection Info

tools.description: Reconnect this MCP server to another configured profile by name.

db.description: Alias for profile for compatibility with older clients. Prefer profile.

tools.name: oracle_switch_profile
tools.title: Oracle Switch Profile

tools.description: Preview or apply a temporary session operating-level elevation within the active profile ceiling, or drop back to READ_ONLY.

action.description: Optional action: preview, apply, drop, or status. Omit for preview/apply based on execute.

confirm.description: Confirmation token returned by preview. Required when execute=true raises the level.

execute.description: Default false previews the level change and returns a confirmation token. Set true with confirm to apply elevation.

level.description: Target level: READ_WRITE, DDL, or ADMIN. READ_ONLY drops any active elevation.

ttl_seconds.description: Temporary elevation window in seconds (default 900, hard cap 3600).

tools.name: oracle_set_session_level
tools.title: Oracle Set Session Level

tools.description: Run a read-only SELECT with positional binds; paginated and row/byte capped.

allow_plan_table_write.description: Default false. Must be true, with the active session at READ_WRITE, before max_query_cost may run EXPLAIN PLAN and write PLAN_TABLE for pre-execution cost estimation.

as_of.description: Flashback / AS-OF read: run this SELECT against a PAST committed snapshot. Pass a NORMAL SELECT (never hand-written AS OF SQL); the base query is proven read-only first, then bounded in a DBMS_FLASHBACK window (the scn/timestamp is bound, never interpolated). Set exactly one of scn or timestamp. Requires the Oracle FLASHBACK privilege (a missing grant surfaces as ORA-01031).

scn.description: System change number to read as of (the deterministic form).

timestamp.description: Wall-clock time to read as of, "YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS" (a T date/time separator is also accepted). Oracle resolves it to the nearest SCN (~3s granularity).

binds.description: Positional bind values (string | number | bool | null) for :1, :2 …

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a prior truncated page (incremental fetch). Resuming with it yields the next page byte-identically.

deep_decode.description: Opt into larger capped ARRAY/JSON/VECTOR structured decode limits. Default false keeps safe caps.

format.description: Inline result format. json (default) returns rows; arrow returns base64 Arrow IPC in arrow_ipc_b64 after the identical masking and audit path. Arrow and export/streaming are mutually exclusive.

limit.description: Alias for max_rows for compatibility with older clients. Prefer max_rows.

max_blob_bytes.description: Maximum BLOB bytes to inline per cell as base64 (default 1048576).

max_col_width.description: Compatibility text cap for ordinary text/raw columns. Truncated values are returned as { value, truncated, char_length }.

max_lob_chars.description: Maximum CLOB characters to inline per cell (default 32768).

max_query_cost.description: Optional per-call optimizer-cost ceiling for this oracle_query call. It can only lower the active profile's max_query_cost. When an effective ceiling exists, oracle_query first runs an EXPLAIN PLAN diagnostic write and refuses before execution if the estimated root cost is unavailable or above the ceiling.

max_result_bytes.description: Maximum compact JSON bytes across row objects in this page (default 10485760, hard cap 26214400; excludes columns, pagination metadata, and the outer MCP envelope).

max_rows.description: Maximum rows in this page / streamed chunk (default 200, hard cap 5000).

max_structured_bytes.description: Maximum compact JSON bytes decoded from one structured node. Values above the safe default require deep_decode=true.

max_structured_cells.description: Maximum structured value nodes decoded from one structured cell. Values above the safe default require deep_decode=true.

max_structured_depth.description: Maximum ARRAY/JSON recursion depth decoded inside one structured cell. Values above the safe default require deep_decode=true.

max_structured_rows.description: Maximum direct entries decoded from one structured ARRAY/JSON node. Values above the safe default require deep_decode=true.

numbers_as_float.description: Emit numeric values as JSON numbers where possible. Default false preserves Oracle NUMBER losslessly as strings.

read_only_standby.description: If true, refuse the max_query_cost estimation path because EXPLAIN PLAN writes PLAN_TABLE. Only meaningful when an effective max_query_cost is set. Defaults false.

sql.description: A single read-only SELECT. Use :1, :2 … for binds.

streaming.description: Deliver the result incrementally instead of one inline page. Over HTTP/SSE, scalar/self-contained rowsets emit one `event: row` frame per row; LOB, BFILE, and REF CURSOR values fall back to ordered cursor `event: chunk` frames. Mutually exclusive with export and as_of. Never affects the read-only classifier.

timeout_seconds.description: Tightens the profile's total request deadline and each Oracle round-trip cap for this tool call. It cannot widen the profile ceiling.

tools.name: oracle_query

arrow_cell_encoding.description: Arrow cell encoding used by arrow_ipc_b64. Decode each UTF-8 value as a JSON literal to recover the same value as JSON mode.

arrow_ipc_b64.description: Base64 Apache Arrow IPC stream for the already-masked, audit-bound result page. Each UTF-8 cell is a JSON literal; see arrow_cell_encoding.

export.description: Present when export=true: metadata for the materialized oracle-export://{id} resource, readable only by the originating principal under the exact scope grant.

format.description: Present as arrow when oracle_query was requested with format=arrow. In that form rows is omitted and the governed page is carried in arrow_ipc_b64.

inlined.description: False when the result was materialized as an export resource (export=true) rather than inlined.

mask_certificate.description: Present when result masking transformed the page: proof-carrying per-column mask decisions plus the audit entry hash binding those decisions into the audit chain.

resource_link.description: Present when export=true: an MCP resource_link to the export, to fetch via resources/read.

rows.description: Serialized rows keyed by column name. NUMBER cells are strings unless numbers_as_float=true.

additionalProperties.description: Serialized Oracle cell value. Oracle NUMBER values are strings by default for lossless precision; JSON numbers are used only for binary float/double or numbers_as_float=true.

tools.title: Oracle Query

tools.description: Run a bounded, fail-closed 23ai vector search through the same policy, semantic-resolution, masking, and audit path as oracle_query.

filter.description: Optional governed hybrid predicate. Only equality is supported: the server validates column as a simple identifier and binds value; raw SQL, OR/IN fragments, and caller-provided operators are refused.

column.description: One local table column to constrain.

value.description: One scalar value bound into the equality predicate; never SQL text.

over.description: The local table and VECTOR column to search. Identifiers must be simple unquoted names and are live-resolved before execution.

column.description: VECTOR column used only for distance ordering.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; defaults to the current schema.

query_text.description: Embedded only by exactly one visible local ONNX model after COMPATIBLE >= 23.4 is proven. Otherwise returns typed requires_23ai or no_in_db_model; it never falls back to client embedding.

query_vector.description: Numeric query vector. Exactly one of query_vector or query_text is required in this tool generation.

timeout_seconds.description: Tightens the profile's total request deadline and each Oracle round-trip cap for this tool call. It cannot widen the profile ceiling.

tools.name: oracle_semantic_search

arrow_cell_encoding.description: Arrow cell encoding used by arrow_ipc_b64. Decode each UTF-8 value as a JSON literal to recover the same value as JSON mode.

arrow_ipc_b64.description: Base64 Apache Arrow IPC stream for the already-masked, audit-bound result page. Each UTF-8 cell is a JSON literal; see arrow_cell_encoding.

export.description: Present when export=true: metadata for the materialized oracle-export://{id} resource, readable only by the originating principal under the exact scope grant.

format.description: Present as arrow when oracle_query was requested with format=arrow. In that form rows is omitted and the governed page is carried in arrow_ipc_b64.

inlined.description: False when the result was materialized as an export resource (export=true) rather than inlined.

mask_certificate.description: Present when result masking transformed the page: proof-carrying per-column mask decisions plus the audit entry hash binding those decisions into the audit chain.

resource_link.description: Present when export=true: an MCP resource_link to the export, to fetch via resources/read.

rows.description: Serialized rows keyed by column name. NUMBER cells are strings unless numbers_as_float=true.

additionalProperties.description: Serialized Oracle cell value. Oracle NUMBER values are strings by default for lossless precision; JSON numbers are used only for binary float/double or numbers_as_float=true.

used_index.description: Null when this bounded read did not inspect an execution plan; never inferred from a requested index.

tools.title: Oracle Semantic Search

tools.description: Diff one proven read-only SELECT across two Oracle SCNs, or across two databases.

binds.description: Positional bind values (string | number | bool | null) for :1, :2 …

key.description: Optional result-column names used to align rows and report changed[]. If omitted, oracle_diff infers a primary key only for a single resolved local table; otherwise it falls back to keyless add/remove.

max_blob_bytes.description: Maximum BLOB bytes to inline per cell as base64 (default 1048576).

max_col_width.description: Compatibility text cap for ordinary text/raw columns. Truncated values are returned as { value, truncated, char_length }.

max_lob_chars.description: Maximum CLOB characters to inline per cell (default 32768).

max_result_bytes.description: Maximum compact JSON bytes per compared side (default 10485760, hard cap 26214400).

max_rows.description: Maximum rows compared from each side (default 200, hard cap 5000).

numbers_as_float.description: Emit numeric values as JSON numbers where possible. Default false preserves Oracle NUMBER losslessly as strings.

profile_a.description: Connection profile for side A. Supplying profile_a and profile_b selects cross-database mode: the same proven read runs against two databases (e.g. prod vs staging), each classified against its own catalog and masked under its own egress policy. Only profiles this server exposes can be named.

profile_b.description: Connection profile for side B. See profile_a.

scn_a.description: System change number for side A. Required with scn_b when comparing one database at two points in time; optional in cross-database mode, where it pins side A to a flashback read instead of the current committed state.

scn_b.description: System change number for side B. See scn_a.

sql.description: A single read-only SELECT/WITH. The same proven SQL is run on both compared sides; it is classified as a read against each database it runs on.

timeout_seconds.description: Tightens the profile's total request deadline and each Oracle round-trip cap for this tool call. It cannot widen the profile ceiling.

tools.name: oracle_diff

added.description: Rows present on side B but not on side A.

additionalProperties.description: Serialized Oracle cell value. Oracle NUMBER values are strings by default for lossless precision; JSON numbers are used only for binary float/double or numbers_as_float=true.

changed.description: Key-aligned row changes; empty for keyless diffs.

additionalProperties.description: Serialized Oracle cell value. Oracle NUMBER values are strings by default for lossless precision; JSON numbers are used only for binary float/double or numbers_as_float=true.
Serialized Oracle cell value. Oracle NUMBER values are strings by default for lossless precision; JSON numbers are used only for binary float/double or numbers_as_float=true.
Serialized Oracle cell value. Oracle NUMBER values are strings by default for lossless precision; JSON numbers are used only for binary float/double or numbers_as_float=true.

columns.description: Column names in the compared query shape.

key_columns.description: Key columns used for row alignment. Empty means keyless multiset diff.

keyed.description: True when rows were aligned by explicit/inferred key columns and changed[] is meaningful.

mask_certificates.description: Present when result masking transformed either flashback page: before/after mask-decision certificates with audit entry hashes.

removed.description: Rows present on side A but not on side B.

additionalProperties.description: Serialized Oracle cell value. Oracle NUMBER values are strings by default for lossless precision; JSON numbers are used only for binary float/double or numbers_as_float=true.

source_a.description: Where side A was read from.

profile.description: Connection profile this side was read from, for a cross-database diff.

scn.description: System change number this side was read as of, when it was a flashback read.

source_b.description: Where side B was read from.

profile.description: Connection profile this side was read from, for a cross-database diff.

scn.description: System change number this side was read as of, when it was a flashback read.

truncated.description: True when either compared page hit a row/byte cap before all rows were compared.

tools.title: Oracle Diff

tools.description: Classify a SQL statement and report whether it would pass the active profile/session gate without executing it.

sql.description: SQL statement to classify. It is never executed.

tools.name: oracle_preview_sql
tools.title: Oracle Preview SQL

tools.description: List schemas that own objects visible to this session, optionally filtered by name.

limit.description: Alias for max_rows for compatibility with older clients. Prefer max_rows.

max_rows.description: Maximum schemas to return (default 200, hard cap 5000).

name_like.description: Optional SQL LIKE pattern for schema names (case-insensitive), e.g. APP%.

tools.name: oracle_list_schemas
tools.title: Oracle List Schemas

tools.description: List objects in the current schema, one owner, or all accessible schemas, with optional type/name filters.

limit.description: Alias for max_rows for compatibility with older clients. Prefer max_rows.

max_rows.description: Maximum objects to return (default 500, hard cap 5000).

name_like.description: Optional SQL LIKE pattern for object_name, e.g. EMP%.

object_type.description: Optional object type filter, e.g. TABLE, VIEW, PACKAGE.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Omit for current schema; use * for all accessible schemas.

tools.name: oracle_schema_inspect
tools.title: Oracle Schema Inspect

tools.description: Unified read-only object search/inspection with a detail_level. names=identifiers only; summary=+column count, comments, and the optimizer ALL_TABLES.NUM_ROWS row-count ESTIMATE (gathered statistics, never COUNT(*) — may be stale, reported via stats_stale/last_analyzed); standard (default)=+columns; full=+indexes. fleet=true searches the names-only index across MCP-visible profiles, applying each source profile's egress policy before merge and returning no profile roster or profile counts. Returns {count, results, truncated}. Owner/type/name filters are bound; quoted/case-sensitive identifiers are matched verbatim.

detail_level.description: Enrichment level. names=identifiers only; summary=+column count + comments + the optimizer ALL_TABLES.NUM_ROWS estimate (NOT COUNT(*)); standard (default)=+columns; full=+indexes.

fleet.description: Search the egress-safe unified fleet catalog. Fleet mode requires detail_level=names, applies each profile's own result-masking policy before aggregation, and intentionally returns no profile roster or profile-count metadata.

limit.description: Alias for max_rows for compatibility with older clients. Prefer max_rows.

max_rows.description: Maximum objects to return (default 100, hard cap 5000).

name_like.description: Optional SQL LIKE pattern for object_name, e.g. EMP%.

object_type.description: Optional object type filter, e.g. TABLE, VIEW, PACKAGE.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive for ordinary identifiers; quoted identifiers match verbatim). Omit or use * for all accessible schemas.

tools.name: oracle_search_objects
tools.title: Oracle Search Objects

tools.description: Return bounded orientation evidence: by default one cacheable snapshot for the active profile; fleet=true maps every MCP-visible profile independently with schema, version, freshness, drift, and typed UNREACHABLE/FAIL_CLOSED lane status. Dictionary reads are parameterized and read-only; include selects schema, fks, hot, freshness, and/or ddl (all by default). Every selected row list is capped and the response makes truncation explicit.

cursor.description: Opaque continuation cursor returned by a truncated orient response. It is bound to this owner, include selection, fleet flag, and profile.

fleet.description: When true, orient every MCP-visible configured profile independently. Each profile is returned with REACHABLE, UNREACHABLE, or FAIL_CLOSED status; one unavailable lane never omits or fails the other lanes.

include.description: Optional sections to return. Defaults to every section; ddl selects the recent_ddl field.

max_rows.description: Maximum rows for each selected orientation list (default 100). The response sets truncated=true and returns next_cursor when another bounded page may exist.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Omit or use * for all visible schemas.

tools.name: oracle_orient
tools.title: Oracle Orient

tools.description: Describe a table/view's columns and constraint metadata.

max_rows.description: Maximum constraint rows to return (default 200, hard cap 5000).

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema when available.

table.description: Table or view name. May be OWNER.TABLE. Required unless table_name or name is supplied.

table_name.description: Alias for table for compatibility with older clients. Prefer table.

tools.name: oracle_describe
tools.title: Oracle Describe

tools.description: Describe one index's metadata, indexed columns, and function-based expressions.

index_name.description: Alias for name for compatibility with older clients. Prefer name.

name.description: Index name (case-insensitive). Required unless index_name is supplied.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema when available.

tools.name: oracle_describe_index
tools.title: Oracle Describe Index

tools.description: Describe one trigger's timing, event, target table, status, and body.

name.description: Trigger name (case-insensitive). Required unless trigger_name is supplied.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema when available.

trigger_name.description: Alias for name for compatibility with older clients. Prefer name.

tools.name: oracle_describe_trigger
tools.title: Oracle Describe Trigger

tools.description: Describe one view's definition metadata and columns.

name.description: View name (case-insensitive). Required unless view_name is supplied.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema when available.

view_name.description: Alias for name for compatibility with older clients. Prefer name.

tools.name: oracle_describe_view
tools.title: Oracle Describe View

tools.description: Fetch an object's DDL via DBMS_METADATA.GET_DDL (allowlisted object types). Responses include full and returned character counts plus an explicit truncated flag; a long DDL prefix is never presented as a complete document.

name.description: Object name. May be OWNER.NAME. Required unless object_name is supplied.

object_name.description: Alias for name for compatibility with older clients. Prefer name.

object_type.description: Allowlisted type, e.g. TABLE, VIEW, PACKAGE, PACKAGE_BODY, PROCEDURE, FUNCTION, TRIGGER, TYPE, SEQUENCE, INDEX, SYNONYM.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema when available.

tools.name: oracle_get_ddl
tools.title: Oracle Get DDL

tools.description: Fetch an object's full source text or inclusive line range from ALL_SOURCE with a character cap. Omit object_type to return every visible source variant for the object name.

from_line.description: Optional first ALL_SOURCE line to return, inclusive. Pair with oracle_search_source's LINE result.

name.description: Object name. May be OWNER.NAME. Required unless object_name is supplied.

object_name.description: Alias for name for compatibility with older clients. Prefer name.

object_type.description: Optional supported source type: PACKAGE, PACKAGE_BODY, PROCEDURE, FUNCTION, TRIGGER, TYPE, TYPE_BODY. When omitted, all visible source types for this name are returned.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema when available.

to_line.description: Optional last ALL_SOURCE line to return, inclusive. Must not be lower than from_line.

tools.name: oracle_get_source
tools.title: Oracle Get Source

tools.description: Read the first rows of a table or view with a hard row cap.

limit.description: Alias for max_rows for compatibility with older clients. Prefer max_rows.

max_rows.description: Maximum rows to return (default 50, hard cap 1000).

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema when available.

table.description: Table or view name. May be OWNER.TABLE. Required unless table_name is supplied.

table_name.description: Alias for table for compatibility with older clients. Prefer table.

tools.name: oracle_sample_rows
tools.title: Oracle Sample Rows

tools.description: Read one CLOB/NCLOB/text value by key with a character cap.

clob_column.description: CLOB/NCLOB/text column name (case-insensitive). Required unless clob_col is supplied.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema when available.

pk_column.description: Key column name (case-insensitive). Required unless pk_col is supplied.

pk_value.description: Key value bound as :1. Required unless pk_val is supplied.

table.description: Table or view name. May be OWNER.TABLE. Required unless table_name is supplied.

table_name.description: Alias for table for compatibility with older clients. Prefer table.

tools.name: oracle_read_clob
tools.title: Oracle Read CLOB

tools.description: Retrieve compile errors for the current schema, an owner, or one object (ALL_ERRORS).

max_rows.description: Maximum compile errors to return (default 200, hard cap 5000).

name.description: Optional object name. May be OWNER.NAME. Omit to list all compile errors for the owner/current schema.

object_name.description: Alias for name for compatibility with older clients. Prefer name.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema when available.

tools.name: oracle_compile_errors
tools.title: Oracle Compile Errors

tools.description: Full-text search across ALL_SOURCE for a needle (row- and line-capped).

limit.description: Alias for max_rows for compatibility with older clients. Prefer max_rows.

max_line_chars.description: Maximum characters retained from each matching source line (default 500). Longer lines end with an explicit truncation marker; use oracle_get_source with from_line/to_line for the exact source range.

max_rows.description: Maximum matching source lines to return (default 200, hard cap 5000).

name_like.description: Optional SQL LIKE pattern for source object names, e.g. EMP%.

needle.description: Case-insensitive substring to find in source text.

object_type.description: Optional source type filter: PACKAGE, PACKAGE_BODY, PROCEDURE, FUNCTION, TRIGGER, TYPE, TYPE_BODY.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema; use * for all visible source.

tools.name: oracle_search_source
tools.title: Oracle Search Source

tools.description: Inspect PL/Scope identifier and SQL statement metadata for one PL/SQL object when ALL_IDENTIFIERS/ALL_STATEMENTS are populated.

max_rows.description: Maximum identifiers and SQL statements per array (default 200, hard cap 5000).

name.description: Object name. May be OWNER.NAME. Required unless object_name is supplied.

object_name.description: Alias for name for compatibility. Prefer name.

owner.description: Optional schema owner (case-insensitive). Defaults to current schema when available.

tools.name: oracle_plscope_inspect
tools.title: Oracle PL/Scope Inspect

tools.description: Read-only top-SQL ranked by elapsed/CPU/buffer-gets/disk-reads over the free live cursor cache (V$SQLSTATS). Opt into historical AWR only when a Diagnostics Pack is licensed, else Statspack, else a structured-unavailable error — never invokes a paid pack unlicensed.

historical.description: If true, use historical AWR (requires a licensed Diagnostics Pack) or Statspack instead of the live cursor cache. Defaults false (the free live source).

min_pct_of_total.description: Live source only: keep only statements consuming at least this percent of the total selected metric (e.g. 5 for the 5%-of-total view).

timeout_seconds.description: Tightens the profile's total request deadline and each Oracle round-trip cap for this tool call. It cannot widen the profile ceiling.

top_n.description: How many statements to return (1-100, default 20).

tools.name: oracle_top_queries
tools.title: Oracle Top Queries

tools.description: Read-only historical optimizer plan and relative-cost timeline from AWR snapshots for one SQL ID. Requires a licensed Oracle Diagnostics Pack; when the license probe cannot prove DIAGNOSTIC access it returns a typed refusal before touching DBA_HIST_*.

max_points.description: Maximum AWR snapshot observations to return (default 100, capped at 1000).

sql_id.description: The 13-character Oracle SQL ID to inspect.

timeout_seconds.description: Tightens the profile's total request deadline and each Oracle round-trip cap for this tool call. It cannot widen the profile ceiling.

tools.name: oracle_plan_timeline
tools.title: Oracle Plan Timeline

tools.description: Read-only DBA health-check suite. health_type='all' (default) or a comma list of subchecks (invalid_objects, unusable_indexes, tablespace_undo, sequence_ceiling, disabled_constraints, buffer_cache_hit_ratio). Pure V$/DBA_*/ALL_* reads; each subcheck degrades DBA_*->ALL_* and yields a structured skip on insufficient privilege rather than failing the suite.

health_type.description: Either 'all' (default) or a comma-separated list of subcheck names: invalid_objects, unusable_indexes, tablespace_undo, sequence_ceiling, disabled_constraints, buffer_cache_hit_ratio. Unknown names are reported, not fatal.

timeout_seconds.description: Tightens the profile's total request deadline and each Oracle round-trip cap for this tool call. It cannot widen the profile ceiling.

tools.name: oracle_db_health
tools.title: Oracle Db Health

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_connection_info; returns connected=false with recovery hints when live connection metadata is unavailable.
tools.name: current_database
tools.title: Current Database

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_switch_profile; pass a configured profile name as db or profile.

db.description: Configured profile name. Alias for profile.

tools.name: switch_database
tools.title: Switch Database

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_query.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a prior truncated page (incremental fetch).

deep_decode.description: Opt into larger capped ARRAY/JSON/VECTOR structured decode limits. Default false keeps safe caps.

export.description: When true, materialize the bounded full result as an oracle-export://{id} resource and return a resource_link instead of inlining rows. The resource is bound to the originating principal and exact scope grant.

export_format.description: Export serialization when export=true: csv (default) or json.

limit.description: Alias for max_rows for compatibility with older clients. Prefer max_rows.

max_blob_bytes.description: Maximum BLOB bytes to inline per cell as base64.

max_col_width.description: Compatibility text cap for ordinary text/raw columns.

max_lob_chars.description: Maximum CLOB characters to inline per cell.

max_result_bytes.description: Maximum compact JSON bytes across row objects in this page; excludes columns, pagination metadata, and the outer MCP envelope.

max_rows.description: Maximum rows in this page (default 200, hard cap 5000).

max_structured_bytes.description: Maximum compact JSON bytes decoded from one structured node. Values above the safe default require deep_decode=true.

max_structured_cells.description: Maximum structured value nodes decoded from one structured cell. Values above the safe default require deep_decode=true.

max_structured_depth.description: Maximum ARRAY/JSON recursion depth decoded inside one structured cell. Values above the safe default require deep_decode=true.

max_structured_rows.description: Maximum direct entries decoded from one structured ARRAY/JSON node. Values above the safe default require deep_decode=true.

numbers_as_float.description: Emit numeric values as JSON numbers where possible.

sql.description: A single read-only SELECT. Use :1, :2 ... for binds.

streaming.description: Deliver the result incrementally instead of one inline page. Over HTTP/SSE, scalar/self-contained rowsets emit one `event: row` frame per row; LOB, BFILE, and REF CURSOR values fall back to ordered cursor `event: chunk` frames. Mutually exclusive with export and as_of. Never affects the read-only classifier.

timeout_seconds.description: Tightens the profile's total request deadline and each Oracle round-trip cap for this tool call. It cannot widen the profile ceiling.

tools.name: query

arrow_cell_encoding.description: Arrow cell encoding used by arrow_ipc_b64. Decode each UTF-8 value as a JSON literal to recover the same value as JSON mode.

arrow_ipc_b64.description: Base64 Apache Arrow IPC stream for the already-masked, audit-bound result page. Each UTF-8 cell is a JSON literal; see arrow_cell_encoding.

export.description: Present when export=true: metadata for the materialized oracle-export://{id} resource, readable only by the originating principal under the exact scope grant.

format.description: Present as arrow when oracle_query was requested with format=arrow. In that form rows is omitted and the governed page is carried in arrow_ipc_b64.

inlined.description: False when the result was materialized as an export resource (export=true) rather than inlined.

mask_certificate.description: Present when result masking transformed the page: proof-carrying per-column mask decisions plus the audit entry hash binding those decisions into the audit chain.

resource_link.description: Present when export=true: an MCP resource_link to the export, to fetch via resources/read.

rows.description: Serialized rows keyed by column name. NUMBER cells are strings unless numbers_as_float=true.

additionalProperties.description: Serialized Oracle cell value. Oracle NUMBER values are strings by default for lossless precision; JSON numbers are used only for binary float/double or numbers_as_float=true.

tools.title: Query

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_preview_sql.

sql.description: SQL statement to classify. It is never executed.

tools.name: preview_sql
tools.title: Preview SQL

tools.description: Compatibility helper for reading the last in-memory source patch preview returned by oracle_patch_source, patch_package, or patch_view.

max_chars.description: Maximum DDL preview characters to return for one object (default 100000).

name.description: Optional object name to inspect. If omitted, lists remembered patch previews for the active profile.

tools.name: read_patch_preview
tools.title: Read Patch Preview

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_schema_inspect.

name_like.description: Optional SQL LIKE pattern for object_name.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; omit for current schema, or use * for all accessible schemas.

tools.name: list_objects
tools.title: List Objects

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_list_schemas.

name_like.description: Optional SQL LIKE pattern for schema names.

tools.name: list_schemas
tools.title: List Schemas

tools.description: Compact schema orientation alias. With no object_type it lists TABLE, VIEW, and PACKAGE objects only; a bounded truncated page carries an opaque cursor.

cursor.description: Opaque continuation cursor returned by a truncated get_schema page.

limit.description: Maximum objects to return (default 100, hard cap 250).

name_like.description: Optional SQL LIKE pattern for object_name.

object_type.description: Optional object type filter. Supplying it overrides the compact TABLE/VIEW/PACKAGE default projection.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; omit for current schema, or use * for all accessible schemas.

tools.name: get_schema
tools.title: Get Schema

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_describe; returns columns and constraints.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; defaults to current schema.

table_name.description: Table or view name. May be OWNER.TABLE.

tools.name: describe_table
tools.title: Describe Table

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_describe_index.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; defaults to current schema.

tools.name: describe_index
tools.title: Describe Index

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_describe_trigger.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; defaults to current schema.

tools.name: describe_trigger
tools.title: Describe Trigger

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_describe_view.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; defaults to current schema.

tools.name: describe_view
tools.title: Describe View

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_get_ddl; long DDL is explicitly marked partial rather than silently truncated.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; defaults to current schema.

tools.name: get_ddl
tools.title: Get DDL

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_get_source. Omit object_type to return every visible source variant for the object name.

object_type.description: Optional source type: PACKAGE, PACKAGE_BODY, PROCEDURE, FUNCTION, TRIGGER, TYPE, or TYPE_BODY. When omitted, all visible source types for this name are returned.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; defaults to current schema.

tools.name: get_object_source
tools.title: Get Object Source

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_compile_errors.

object_name.description: Optional object name. May be OWNER.NAME.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; defaults to current schema.

tools.name: get_errors
tools.title: Get Errors

tools.description: Compatibility alias for oracle_read_clob.

owner.description: Optional schema owner; defaults to current schema.

table.description: Table or view name. May be OWNER.TABLE.

tools.name: get_clob
tools.title: Get CLOB
